summaries.source_file: meghalaya/minutes/Minutes of the SLBC meeting for qtr. ending December 2022.txt
summaries.summary:
**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 25% and poor ACP performance must extend priority sector loans to achieve ACP targets by March 31, 2023
- Six-month financial inclusion campaign launched in Ribhoi District (February-August 2023) to saturate account opening, social security enrollment, and Mudra loan coverage
- Nine districts identified with low priority sector lending must address this through DCC/DLRC meetings
- Banks directed to focus lending on agri-allied activities like fishery, piggery, dairy and horticulture

**Performance Highlights**
- State economy showed resilience with deposit growth of 7.22% and advances growth of 11.98% year-on-year
- CD ratio improved from 43.75% (Dec 2021) to 45.69% (Dec 2022), though still below RBI benchmark
- Priority sector lending at 43.07% exceeded the 40% benchmark requirement
- High concern over PMEGP NPA rate at 41.23%, requiring immediate recovery efforts

**Action Items**
- All banks to clear pending PMEGP loan proposals by March 31, 2023 and improve NPA recovery with government support
- LDMs in nine districts to prioritize low priority sector lending discussions in meetings
- Banks to maximize credit linkage for RSETI trainees and process proposals in time-bound manner
- Reactivate inactive Business Correspondents and improve digital transaction volumes

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 25% (Jana Small Finance Bank, South Indian Bank, IDFC Bank, Union Bank, Axis Bank, and Indian Bank) must submit action plans to improve their ratios or face withdrawal of government deposits
- State government to achieve $10 billion economy target by 2027-28 with increased bank credit flow to priority sectors
- All banks directed to prioritize agriculture lending, especially KCC (Kisan Credit Cards), to improve the state's overall CD ratio of 45.82%
- 100% coverage mandate for social security schemes (PMJJBY, PMSBY, APY) by June 30, 2023

**Performance Highlights**
- Meghalaya served by 32 banks with 434 branches, 497 ATMs, and 1,377 Business Correspondents for 40 lakh population
- PMEGP achieved 77.65% target with 893 sanctioned proposals against 1,151 target, but margin money claims lagged at Rs.6.67 crore out of Rs.24.35 crore
- SHG credit linkage sanctioned 6,702 proposals in 2022-23, with new target set at 18,000 SHGs worth Rs.210 crore for 2023-24

**Action Items**
- Agriculture Department to review Aadhar card mandatory requirement for interest subvention and collect bank-wise KCC data
- PNB to expedite opening of pending RSETI in Jaintia Hills district after two-year delay
- KVIC and banks to coordinate on timely EDP training for PMEGP beneficiaries and complete sanctions by December 2023

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 25% (Jana Small Finance Bank, South Indian Bank, IDFC, Union Bank, Axis Bank, and Indian Bank) must submit action plans to improve their ratios or face withdrawal of government deposits
- State Government targeting to make Meghalaya a $10 billion economy by 2027-28, requiring increased bank credit flow to priority sectors
- Banks directed to prioritize agriculture lending, especially KCC loans, with focus on institutions having less than 25% CD ratio
- Three-month campaign launched (April-June 2023) to achieve 100% coverage under social security schemes (PMJJBY, PMSBY, APY)

**Performance Highlights**
- Meghalaya served by 32 banks with 434 branches, 497 ATMs, and 1,377 BCs for 40 lakh population, but CD ratio stands at only 45.82%
- PMEGP achieved 77.65% target with 893 loan proposals sanctioned against 1,151 target during 2022-23
- 6,702 SHG loan proposals sanctioned in 2022-23; new target set for 18,000 SHGs worth ₹210 crore in 2023-24

**Action Items**
- Agriculture Department to review Aadhaar card mandate for interest subvention and coordinate with banks on KCC data collection
- KVIC and banks to expedite PMEGP proposal sanctions by December 2023 to complete margin money claims by March 2024
- PNB to accelerate establishment of pending RSETI in Jaintia Hills district after two-year delay

**Key Decisions**
- All banks must complete branch openings as per allotment to achieve financial inclusion across Meghalaya's districts
- SBI and UCO Bank directed to increase cash retention limits at Ranikor and Dangar-Balat branches respectively to enable timely government scheme payments
- Axis Bank must immediately open branch at unbanked Rongara Block; SBI requested to explore re-opening its branch there
- Private banks instructed to open BPL accounts with zero balance only

**Performance Highlights**
- Customer Service Points increased significantly from 1,154 (Sept 2022) to 1,894 (Sept 2023)
- Annual Credit Plan achievement reached ₹1,338.79 crores (50.28% of annual target) by Q2
- 12 banks, including major ones like BoB, MCAB, MRB, UCO, ICICI and CBI, achieved less than 25% of ACP targets
- Five banks maintain concerning CD ratios below 25%

**Action Items**
- All banks to simplify account opening application forms for BPL and no-frills accounts
- SBI to increase manpower at Ranikor branch, upgrade V-Sat connectivity, and deploy more CSPs in villages
- Banks with low ACP achievement must reach allotted targets by financial year-end, particularly improving finance in agriculture, SHG credit linkages, Mudra loans, and housing
- MRB to clarify insurance requirements for PMEGP loans under Homestay scheme to remove customer confusion

**Key Decisions**
- All banks directed to improve Meghalaya's CD ratio from current 50.80% to reach national benchmark of 60%
- Target set for 100% digital payment ecosystem coverage across all districts by December 2024, with quarterly performance reviews
- Matrix analysis framework to be finalized for evaluating bank performance in government schemes and priority sector lending
- Banks instructed to upgrade V-sat connectivity for better internet services at branches

**Performance Highlights**
- Total deposits reached ₹36,359.02 crores and advances ₹18,469.60 crores as of December 2023
- Financial inclusion coverage achieved 6,432 out of 6,459 identified villages through banking outlets and BC model
- Major banks including MRB, ICICI, and HDFC noted for particularly low CD ratios, with MRB affected by sizeable deposits in Jaintia Hills

**Action Items**
- All banks to provide Aadhaar enrollment support and ensure timely PMAY beneficiary payments with additional manpower at block headquarters
- Axis Bank to accelerate branch opening at Rongara (only unbanked block in South Garo Hills); SBI to consider re-opening branch there
- All banks to register with ECI portal for election monitoring and report suspicious transactions during general elections

summaries.filename: Minutes of the SLBC Meeting Mar 24.pdf
summaries.source_file: meghalaya/minutes/Minutes of the SLBC Meeting Mar 24.txt
summaries.summary:
**Key Decisions**
- CD ratio target of 60% emphasized as banks achieved only 50.23% in March 2024, up from 45.82% in 2023
- CM-Connect and CM Elevate schemes launched to improve banking services in remote areas with 30,000 applications processed under CM Elevate
- Matrix application to be developed by NIC, Finance Department and SLBC to measure individual banks' performance in serving local people
- Business Correspondent services standardized with SBI to create template for tracking active/inactive BCs

**Performance Highlights**
- Total deposits reached ₹39,198 crores and advances ₹19,688 crores as of March 2024
- Priority sector lending achieved 43.62% (above 40% RBI benchmark) with MSME sector reaching 128% of target
- Overall priority sector loan achievement was 89.62% (₹2,386 crores against ₹2,663 crores target)

**Action Items**
- Axis Bank to construct prefab branch at Rongara (only unbanked block) with state financial aid
- Multiple banks to resolve premises issues for branch openings: PNB at Baghmara/Ampati, MCAB at Adokgre/Raksamgre/Damalgre
- KVIC to complete EDP training for 91 remaining PMEGP beneficiaries and explore CIBIL access for proposal verification

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 25% must improve to minimum 25% in coming quarters, with those below 40% to discuss this in District Level Consultative Committees
- SLBC to drop redundant agenda items already discussed in sub-committees and present all figures in lakhs format
- Separate meeting to be convened between Finance Department, Industries Department, KVIC and banks to address high PMEGP NPA levels and delayed subsidy settlements

**Performance Highlights**
- Deposits declined by Rs 410.41 crores and advances by Rs 199.23 crores during June 2024 quarter, though CD ratio marginally improved from 50.23% to 50.25%
- Agricultural advances at 16.65% of total advances, below RBI's 18% target, with ACP growth of only 12.41%
- PMEGP NPA reduced from 41.3% (March 2024) to 18.5% (June 2024), primarily through write-offs rather than recoveries

**Action Items**
- Finance Department and NIC to develop Matrix system before next SLBC meeting
- Banks and MSRLS to conduct awareness programs for low-enrolled social security schemes (PMJJBY, PMSBY, APY) despite 80%+ Aadhaar enrollment
- Multiple branch openings to be expedited: MCAB's Ranikor branch (extended to December 2024), MRB's Tikrilla branch (September 2024), and resolution of building issues for Axis Bank's Rongara branch

**Key Decisions**
- Banks directed to increase advances portfolio to achieve national CD ratio benchmark of 60% (currently at 50.69%)
- Chief Secretary mandated banks to show performance in PM Surya Ghar and PM Vishwakarma schemes by next SLBC meeting
- SLBC instructed to include PM SYM (Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maandhan) scheme in future meeting agendas alongside other social security schemes
- Banks urged to focus on KCC saturation program, particularly in Animal Husbandry and Fisheries sectors

**Performance Highlights**
- Deposits grew 8.78% YOY and advances increased 10.42% YOY for September 2024
- Priority sector lending achieved 43.95%, exceeding minimum 40% target, with 16% YOY growth
- Agriculture advances at 17.23% remain below RBI's 18% benchmark
- ACP performance reached 50.77% of target as of September 2024
- NULM NPA at alarming 50% level impacting credit culture

**Action Items**
- Banks to provide details of advances under PM Surya Ghar scheme in next SLBC meeting
- All stakeholders to reduce NPA under NULM scheme through district-level focused efforts
- Banks with zero outstanding in government sponsored schemes must actively participate in flagship programs like PM Vishwakarma, PM Svanidhi, and PMEGP
- Leverage BC/CSP channels to increase enrollment in social security schemes

**Key Decisions**
- Banks must improve Credit Deposit Ratio from current 50.99% as 22 banks fall below state average, with 7 banks having CDR below 20%
- All banks directed to complete pending ATM and branch installations within financial year 2025-26
- Target deadline of September 30, 2025 set for achieving Digital Payment Ecosystem expansion goals
- Banks urged to simplify application forms, reduce documentation requirements, and conduct more financial literacy campaigns at district, block, and village levels

**Performance Highlights**
- Priority Sector Lending stands at ₹8,725 crores (46% of total advances), above RBI's 40% benchmark with 73.7% ACP achievement
- Agriculture lending severely underperforming at 43.60% achievement with 9 banks having nil agriculture lending
- High NPAs in government schemes: NULM (53.35%), PMEGP (17.24%), PMMY (10.48%), though NRLM SHG maintains low 1.93% NPA
- PM Surya Ghar scheme shows minimal progress with only 22 loans sanctioned during quarter; PM Vishwakarma has 5 loans sanctioned from 28 applications

**Action Items**
- SLBC and all banks to focus on increasing loans under flagship schemes PM Surya Ghar and PM Vishwakarma
- Banks to attend final day of PM Vishwakarma training programs to provide loan awareness to applicants
- All banks, especially MRB and MCAB, to develop banking products tailored for local population needs and improve rural credit accessibility

**Key Decisions**
- Special Saturation Campaign for Social Security Schemes (PMJDY, PMSBY, PMJJBY, APY) launched for July-September 2025 with bank-wise targets allocated
- Formation of sub-committee headed by Finance Secretary R. Swer to review campaign performance fortnightly
- All banks directed to achieve 100% digitization of accounts by September 2025 (revised timeline)
- Banks with nil outstanding in agriculture advances must participate to achieve 18% agriculture lending benchmark

**Performance Highlights**  
- Total deposits reached ₹42,195 crores (7.65% YoY growth) and credit portfolio ₹20,856 crores (5.93% growth)
- CD ratio stands at 49.43%, below the target minimum of 60%
- Priority Sector Lending at 44.87% of advances, above 40% benchmark with 8.97% growth
- ACP achievement at 85.23% overall: agriculture 60.16%, MSME 109.78%, other priority sectors 39.53%

**Action Items**
- All banks to focus on KCC, animal husbandry, and fishery financing under saturation programs
- PNB to open priority branch in Dadenggre as identified by DFS
- LDMs and banks to reduce high NPAs in NULM (48.61%) and PMEGP (14.97%) schemes through coordinated district-level efforts

**Key Decisions**
- Set target to open 12 new bank branches during FY 2025-26 (5 pending from previous year + 7 new targets)
- Directed 15 banks with CD ratio below 30% to cross this benchmark in next quarter
- Extended PM Svanidhi scheme till March 2030 for street vendors
- Planned district-wise loan melas for PM Vishwakarma scheme to boost participation

**Performance Highlights**
- State CD ratio improved from 49.43% (March 2025) to 52.31% (June 2025) but remains below 60% benchmark
- Financial inclusion campaign achieved 83% in PMJDY, 81% in PMJJBY, 69% in PMSBY, but only 31% in APY
- PM Surya Ghar scheme saw 124 loans sanctioned in Q1 (50% of cumulative total), but only 2 banks (SBI and MRB) participating
- Priority Sector Lending at 41.62% exceeds 40% minimum, though 3 banks have nil PSL exposure

**Action Items**
- Secretary Finance to conduct separate meeting with banks on pending branch openings
- Axis Bank to open Rongara branch before December 25, 2025
- All banks urged to onboard CM-Elevate scheme (currently only 5 banks participating)
- SLBC and Industries Department to organize district-wise loan melas for PM Vishwakarma scheme

**Key Decisions**
- Chief Secretary warned banks with CD ratio below 30% that government will withdraw deposits if they fail to improve performance in coming quarter
- SLBC to allot a bank to open new branch in Rerapara Block, South West Garo Hills (currently has no banking facility)
- Industries Department instructed to conduct 2-day block-level programs with banks for PM Vishwakarma scheme loan sanctioning
- SLBC to fix bank-wise, branch-wise targets for Atal Pension Yojana with monthly reviews by Finance Department

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved marginally from 52.31% to 52.83% but remains below 60% benchmark
- Priority Sector Advances at 43.94% exceeded 40% RBI minimum requirement
- Banks achieved only 48.56% of Annual ACP target with strong MSME performance (65.82%) but weak Agriculture (25.16%) and Other Priority segments (18.93%)
- PM Surya Ghar scheme saw 386 loans sanctioned (374 by SBI, 12 by MRB), while PM Vishwakarma had 58 loans worth ₹49.65 lakhs

**Action Items**
- Kotak Mahindra Bank and Jana Small Finance Bank to start priority sector lending from next quarter
- All banks to participate in PM Surya Ghar solar loan scheme and improve performance in Agriculture and Other Priority lending segments
- Industries Department to coordinate with banks for applicant training programs and loan sanctioning events at block level

**Key Decisions**
- State Level Financial Inclusion (SLFI) Committee formed with Chief Secretary as Chairman to monitor PMJDY, PMJJBY, APY and PMMY schemes
- All 705 villages in Mizoram divided into 91 SSAs and allocated among 16 banks for financial inclusion coverage
- Banks directed to appoint sufficient CSPs/BCs to cover remaining unbanked villages, with at least 5% of villages under 2000 population requiring brick-and-mortar branches
- Setting up of RUDSETI for Kolasib and Mamit districts assigned to Canara Bank (though proposal was declined by Board of Governors)

**Performance Highlights**
- Only 64 CSPs appointed by various banks out of required coverage, with Federal Bank yet to appoint any BC/CSP
- Out of 533 Weavers Credit Card applications, only 117 sanctioned (SBI-45, MRB-72), with several banks claiming non-receipt of applications
- MRB appointed 23 BCs out of 37 allocated SSAs, SBI appointed 12 BCs out of 27 allocated SSAs

**Action Items**
- Banks to provide village-wise lists of CSPs appointed to Lead Bank Office and explore opening new branches in remaining unbanked villages
- SBI and MRB to expedite appointment of remaining BCs/CSPs for their allocated villages
- Banks to process pending Weavers Credit Card applications and confirm receipt status with concerned departments

**Key Decisions**
- Banks must provide data on time to Lead Bank for error-free background papers and regular SLBC meetings as per RBI calendar
- Weavers Credit Card applications will be submitted directly to bank branches where applicants hold accounts, with acknowledgment receipts required from Industries Department
- RRBs confirmed exempt from sanctioning DRI loans; only commercial banks can provide these ₹20,000 loans at 4% interest for IAY beneficiaries
- Vijaya Bank assigned to prepare detailed report on Business Correspondent effectiveness, including strengths and weaknesses

**Performance Highlights**
- 198 Weavers Credit Cards sanctioned across multiple branches, with SBI Thenzawl leading at 85 sanctions
- Federal Bank completed CSP appointment as planned; MRB appointed 6 additional Business Correspondents awaiting bio-metric machine deployment
- NERLP partnered with SBI to provide POS machines to 18 Bank Facilitators covering village banking services, with plans to extend statewide

**Action Items**
- SBI to extend POS machine facility through NERLP Bank Facilitators to all districts and report results in next meeting
- Industries Department to implement new loan application submission process directly to bank branches with proper acknowledgment system
- Vijaya Bank to submit comprehensive BC performance analysis to Lead Bank for next meeting discussion

**Key Decisions**
- All banks directed to submit accurate daily demonetization reports to Department of Financial Services in a timely manner
- Three district-wide events organized by Lead District Managers focusing on Aadhaar/mobile seeding, RuPay card distribution, and awareness campaigns for six central schemes (PMJDY, PMJJBY, PMSBY, PMMY, SUI, APY)
- Request approved for opening new MRB branch at Sakawrdai, a civil sub-divisional headquarters serving over 100 government employees and 35 Self Help Groups
- Finance and Labour Departments tasked to work out financial implications for state government co-contribution to Atal Pension Yojana

**Performance Highlights**
- Mizoram achieved nearly 100% financial inclusion with over 9 lakh savings accounts opened against a population of less than 11 lakh
- Banking operations stabilized post-demonetization with shorter queues reported at bank branches
- Special drive for financial inclusion successfully conducted from September 15 to October 31, 2016

**Action Items**
- All banks to upload error-free data within stipulated timeframes for future SLBC meetings
- Labour Department to be included in next SLBC meeting to discuss APY implementation
- Finance and Labour Departments to calculate financial implications of state co-contribution for construction workers, MGNREGA workers, and other unorganized sector employees under APY scheme

**Key Decisions**
- SLBC data upload timeline fixed at 30 days after quarter end; banks failing to comply will have previous quarter data repeated with asterisk marks
- Action-oriented minutes format adopted with specific bank consultations for compliance tracking
- All banks mandated to finance eligible applications under government schemes without collateral security for loans up to Rs. 3 lakh as per RBI guidelines

**Performance Highlights**
- SHGs performance extremely poor: only 33 loans (Rs. 27.30 lakh) sanctioned against 326 target under NERLP; 27 loans (Rs. 13.54 lakh) against 118 target under MzSRLM
- PMEGP achievement at 488 loans with Rs. 488.42 lakh margin money against annual target of 655 loans and Rs. 1310.74 lakh
- Ten banks including Central Bank, Syndicate Bank, ICICI, Axis, HDFC, and others had sanctioned zero loans under PMEGP

**Action Items**
- MRB Head Office to issue instructions to branches for NRLM/NERLP loan sanctions since most SHGs bank with MRB branches
- All non-performing banks under PMEGP to sanction loans within the month and achieve targets
- Banks to finance all eligible applicants under government-sponsored schemes including NULM sourced by MzSUL

**Key Decisions**
- SLBC meeting held on 19.12.2017 for September 2017 quarter review under Chief Secretary's chairmanship
- Minutes of previous June 2017 quarter meeting adopted without amendments
- RBI instructions implemented for dedicated counters for senior citizens and differently-abled persons, plus door-step banking services for those over 70 years
- New Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) caps set for debit card transactions effective January 1, 2018: 0.40% for small merchants, 0.90% for others

**Performance Highlights**
- Mudra Promotion Campaign successfully conducted on 11.10.2017 with 56 loans sanctioned on camp date and 32 banks/FIs participating
- NRLM progress: 22 loans worth Rs.38.03 lakhs sanctioned in Aizawl district through September 2017, with additional Rs.22.00 lakhs after September
- MzSRLM scheme achieved 22 loans worth Rs.26.90 lakhs by September 2017, but significantly behind annual target of 1137 loans worth Rs.1174.50 lakhs

**Action Items**
- MCAB & MRB to submit interest subvention claims on crop loans by deadline of 25.12.2017
- MRB & NERLP to complete signing of MOU within 2-3 days after corporate office vetting
- All banks requested to accelerate lending to eligible SHGs under MzSRLM to meet ambitious annual targets

**Key Decisions**
- Constituted a 15-member Steering Sub-committee for SLBC to focus meetings on policy issues and delegate routine matters to sub-committees, as per RBI's revamped Lead Bank Scheme guidelines
- Set district-wise targets for NRLM loans totaling 825 loans worth Rs. 4213.50 lakhs across 6 districts for FY 2018-19
- Directed all private banks (Axis, Bandhan, Federal, HDFC, ICICI, IndusInd, South Indian Bank) to start sanctioning PMEGP loans, following RBI intervention

**Performance Highlights**
- Mizoram ranked among top states nationally during Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (GSA) campaign for PMJDY account opening and insurance enrollment
- Under NRLM through MzSRLM, banks sanctioned 301 loans worth Rs. 320.60 lakhs out of 447 applications received during the year
- MOU between Mizoram Rural Bank (MRB) and NERLP was successfully signed on June 26, 2018

**Action Items**
- MRB and SBI to sanction all pending loan proposals from NERLP and MzSRLM at various branches (Darlawn, Tlabung, Bilkhawthlir, Lungdai)
- All nodal agencies to complete PMEGP beneficiary selection by August 2018, with banks to process applications promptly upon receipt
- SLBC to write to SIDBI for MRB's membership in Credit Guarantee Trust Fund for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)

**Key Decisions**
- Banks directed to increase agriculture lending from current 14.5% to minimum 18% in phased manner
- All banks instructed to focus on MSME Outreach programme targets for Serchhip district 
- Decision to filter villages with 1000+ population for priority BC coverage, considering viability constraints in villages below 500 population
- Post Office Payment Banks to accelerate rollout at 20 villages per month to improve financial inclusion coverage

**Performance Highlights**
- Strong priority sector lending at 62% of total advances (above national average), with total deposits of ₹9,205 crores and advances of ₹3,831 crores as of September 2018
- SHG linkages performed well with disbursement of ₹52.50 crores
- Financial inclusion coverage reached 229 out of 389 identified villages, with 291 unbanked rural centers covered under RBI roadmap
- CD ratio stands at 40% but net advances declined by ₹750 crores despite fresh infusion of ₹463 crores

**Action Items**
- Banks to work with government departments to improve agriculture sector lending through proactive measures
- Service providers and government departments to collaborate on improving connectivity infrastructure for better financial inclusion initiatives
- Post Office Payment Banks to continue expansion in villages below 2000 population to support financial inclusion targets

**Key Decisions**
- CD ratio improvement identified as critical priority, with state standing at only 41-42% compared to national averages
- Banks directed to increase priority sector lending, particularly in agriculture which stands at 14% against required 18%
- State government committed to assist banks in loan recovery efforts and facilitate branch expansion with security measures
- Private banks specifically targeted for low CD ratios (some below 10%) and inadequate priority sector exposure

**Performance Highlights**
- PMAY scheme showing progress with 694 loans sanctioned totaling ₹35.40 crores by December 2018
- SBI and MRB achieved 64% coverage with 311 out of 483 touch points operational
- Priority sector lending overall at 62%, though agricultural component lagging significantly
- PMEGP loans facing major challenges with 45% turning NPA

**Action Items**
- Banks to focus on allied agricultural activities (dairy, animal husbandry, fisheries) to boost priority sector lending given land title constraints
- State government to address internet connectivity issues and expedite National Optic Fibre Network implementation
- Trade license issuance process to be streamlined for current account opening
- Enhanced coordination needed between banks and government departments for loan recovery, particularly addressing property auction challenges

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with single-digit CD ratios were directed to increase performance to desired levels, with specific concerns raised about Yes Bank, South Indian Bank, ICICI Bank, Federal Bank, and Axis Bank
- SLBC decided to await RBI clarification on inclusion of IPPB (India Post Payment Bank) access points for covering unbanked villages
- All banks were mandated to achieve their allocated targets for government-sponsored schemes (NRLM, NULM, MUDRA, Stand-Up India, PMEGP) during FY 2019-20
- Banks must inform sponsoring agencies about reasons for loan application rejections under PMAY housing schemes

**Performance Highlights**
- State's overall CD ratio stood at 42%, with priority sector lending at excellent 78.37% against 40% benchmark and agricultural lending at 21.37% against 18% benchmark
- Overall ACP achievement was 67.85% with priority sector lending at 57%, deemed unsatisfactory due to zero achievements by many banks in agriculture and MSME segments
- MSME advances showed negative growth, requiring immediate attention from 10 specified banks including SBI, Axis, and IndusInd

**Action Items**
- RBI to provide clarification on IPPB coverage for unbanked villages and villages with population below 2000
- Banks with zero/nil achievements in agriculture, crop loans, and MSME must report reasons and increase lending in these segments
- All banks to improve enrollment under Social Security Schemes with District Administration support

**Key Decisions**
- Next SLBC meeting scheduled for November 20, 2019, with mandatory attendance by bank heads or branch managers
- Banks must submit roadmaps for providing banking services to remaining 436 unbanked villages by October 31, 2019
- Emergency meeting called for October 10, 2019 to address MSME financing issues after five banks reported zero achievement
- Banking outlet expansion superseded by new roadmap for villages with population below 2000

**Performance Highlights**
- Overall ACP target achievement under priority sector was only 9.07% by June 2019, deemed unsatisfactory
- MSME credit portfolio grew to Rs.988.77 crores (up Rs.122.55 crores from previous year), but five banks showed zero achievement
- Housing for All scheme showed poor performance: only 1,165 loans sanctioned from 14,325 applications sponsored (8.13% approval rate)
- Banking coverage improved: 224 out of 660 villages now have banking services through branches, BCs, or IPPB

**Action Items**
- All banks to come fully prepared to next meeting and provide reasons for poor priority sector lending performance
- MRB to conduct feasibility surveys for new branches at East Phaileng and Kawlbem villages
- Urban Development & Poverty Alleviation Department to provide copies of PMAY application lists to banks for better tracking
- RBI to address Canara Bank Lawngtlai branch's refusal to accept SHG loan applications

**Key Decisions**
- All banks directed to complete coverage of allotted villages under banking services roadmap by December 2019, with progress to be monitored by SLBC
- Committee requested feasibility surveys for ATM installations at Reiek village (SBI), Bethlehem Veng (PNB), and Republic Vengthlang (YES Bank)
- Chief Secretary to address internet connectivity issues with telecom providers; SLBC to write to Power & Electricity Department regarding BC operations

**Performance Highlights**  
- CD ratio improved to 41.50% from 39.70% in previous quarter, with priority sector lending at 66%
- Overall ACP achievement under priority sector reached only 18.99% by September 2019, significantly below the expected 50% target for half-year
- ICICI Bank and YES Bank reported zero achievement under ACP, with YES Bank promising to start priority sector lending from next quarter

**Action Items**
- MRB to conduct fresh feasibility survey for branches at East Phaileng and Kawlbem villages using cluster approach and strengthen staffing at W.Phaileng branch
- UD&PA Department to provide list of Housing For All loan applications to bank controlling offices for proper follow-up
- All banks to appoint Bank Sakhis from SHG members identified by MzSRLM (71 already appointed statewide)

**Key Decisions**
- All banks must complete coverage of allotted villages under the roadmap by opening branches or appointing Business Correspondents by March 2020, with collaboration from MzSRLM for BC appointments
- Committee rejected requests from Central Bank of India, Bank of Baroda, IDBI Bank, and UBI for village reallocation, citing that if HDFC Bank can appoint BC agents in interior areas, other banks should manage similarly
- Banks directed to work harder in remaining 1.5 months to achieve annual credit targets, with special focus on agriculture and crop loans

**Performance Highlights**
- Overall Annual Credit Plan achievement stands at only 28% by December 2019, with agriculture sector severely lagging at 12% and crop loans at mere 2%
- CD ratio declined to 37.77% due to increased deposits from land compensation for road construction
- Priority sector lending comfortable at 55.29%, though agriculture advances remain concerning

**Action Items**
- PNB to open branch and ATM at Bethlehem Veng in next financial year
- YES Bank to complete survey report for Republic Vengthlang ATM by March 2020
- Banks to provide government account information when requested by Accountant General or Finance Department for state fund monitoring

**Key Decisions**
- Banks must complete coverage of all allotted villages under banking services roadmap by September 2020, either through brick-and-mortar branches or Business Correspondents
- Daily progress reports mandated for all banks on Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme disbursements
- Seven banks (Bank of Baroda, Bank of Maharashtra, Federal Bank, IDBI, IndusInd, South Indian Bank, YES Bank) were censured for attending meeting without prior notice

**Performance Highlights**
- Credit-Deposit Ratio of around 40% was deemed unacceptable by the Chief Secretary, who demanded improved lending performance
- Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme showed ₹204.22 crore disbursed across 1,451 accounts by June 2020, with SBI leading at ₹115.30 crore (1,082 accounts)
- Annual Credit Plan achievement under priority sector reached only 34% by March 2020, with agriculture sector particularly weak at 15% achievement

**Action Items**
- All banks to submit daily progress reports on Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme to SLBC
- Specific banks assigned to complete village coverage: Central Bank (Khawlian village), Bank of Baroda (Daido village), IDBI (Lungsei village), UBI (six villages under R.D. Block)
- PNB and YES Bank to submit updates on ATM installations at Bethlehem Veng and Republic Veng

**Key Decisions**
- All banks must complete roadmap coverage of villages with population below 2000 by December 2020 through branches or business correspondents
- Banks directed to focus lending efforts on ECGLS, PM Kisan, and PMSVANidhi schemes during COVID-19 period
- Chairman mandated zero NIL reports from any bank for Central Government Schemes in next quarter
- Special focus required on improving CD ratio, particularly in Aizawl and Hnahthial districts with ratios below 40%

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio declined significantly to 37.09% with negative YoY growth of 18.41% compared to June 2019
- Annual Credit Plan achievement under priority sector only 12.16% by June 2020, with Agriculture at 3% and MSME at 20%
- MSME finance showed positive growth of Rs.102.28 crores, reaching Rs.185.73 crores disbursed
- Multiple banks reported zero achievement in priority sector lending (Indian Bank, ICICI) and various government schemes

**Action Items**
- Indian Bank and ICICI Bank to improve zero achievement under Annual Credit Plan priority sector lending
- 12 banks with negative MSME growth to improve credit disbursement by September 2020 quarter
- MCAB to install ATMs at Bethlehem Veng and Republic Veng following MLA's facility offer
- All banks to collaborate with MzSRLM for business correspondent appointments in unbanked villages

**Key Decisions**
- All banks, particularly FED, ICICI, NESFB, and Yes Bank, directed to increase their Credit-Deposit (CD) ratio which declined from 39.58% in March 2020 to 38.83% in September 2020
- Banks instructed to complete sanction and disbursement of all pending PMEGP and government scheme applications by January 31, 2021
- Chief Secretary mandated banks to improve priority sector lending to achieve 18% benchmark in agriculture through piggery, poultry, fisheries financing

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio decline attributed to COVID-19 pandemic lockdowns and business closures nationwide
- No pending Self Help Group (SHG) linkages reported - all SHGs have been financed by banks
- NPA levels in MSME sector identified as area of concern due to pandemic difficulties, though situation showing improvement

**Action Items**
- All banks to leverage new RBI priority sector guidelines including solar energy loans up to ₹30 crores, FPO loans up to ₹5 crores, and agri-infrastructure loans up to ₹100 crores
- Banks to address technical issues with POS machines at merchant establishments and promote digital payment systems through financial literacy programs
- MzSRLM to accelerate SHG formation despite COVID-19 constraints while following safety protocols

**Key Decisions**
- Banks directed to improve CD ratio by simplifying loan processing mechanisms and funding viable business proposals that lack access to finance
- All banks instructed not to insist on collateral security for government-sponsored scheme loans (DAY-NULM, PMEGP, NRLM) and follow RBI guidelines
- Banks advised not to reject MSME applications due to lack of GST numbers; instead help applicants obtain GSTIN through Tax Department assistance
- Maximum implementation of PM-SVAnidhi scheme for street vendors without hesitation

**Performance Highlights**
- ACP achievement critically low at only 51.41% as of December 31, 2020, with specific concern for BOM, Indian Bank, Axis, ICICI, Indus and Yes Bank performance
- KCC loans showed positive year-over-year growth compared to December 2019
- 425 SHG linkages pending with banks (MRB-372, SBI-2, Canara-17, PNB-1, MCAB-33)

**Action Items**
- All banks to clear pending SHG linkages immediately and MzSRLM to accelerate SHG formation
- Banks to step up priority sector lending and achieve 2020-21 ACP targets, particularly underperforming banks
- RSETI Aizawl to continue skill development programs (conducted 18 programs training 394 candidates April-December 2020)

**Key Decisions**
- All banks, particularly YES Bank, ICICI, SIB and FED Bank, were directed to increase their Credit-Deposit (CD) ratio, with the Chief Secretary urging banks to reach 60% CD ratio within 2-3 years from current 47.45%
- Banks with negative MSME growth (BOM, CBI, IOB, FED and ICICI) were asked to achieve positive growth in the next quarter
- Banks that haven't sanctioned agriculture loans were directed to start financing agriculture and allied activities to achieve the 18% benchmark

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio declined from 48.03% in March 2021 to 47.45% in June 2021, attributed to COVID-19 lockdown and business closures
- Priority Sector advances showed positive achievement of 15.64% in Q1 2021-22 compared to 12.45% in Q1 2020-21
- MUDRA lending showed negative year-on-year growth of Rs.19.52 crores due to the pandemic, with Axis, NESFB and YES Bank recording zero sanctions

**Action Items**
- BOB, Indian Bank, PSB, and Union Bank to improve crop loan performance specifically
- Banks with nil MUDRA sanctions (Axis, NESFB, YES Bank) to begin lending under the scheme in the next quarter
- All banks to enhance lending in government-sponsored schemes and improve priority sector advances through piggery, poultry, fisheries financing

**Key Decisions**
- All banks mandated to improve Credit-Deposit ratio to reach 60% within 2-3 years, with specific focus on YES Bank, SIB, FED, NESFB, ICICI, AXIS, and IDBI
- Banks with zero sanctions under PMMY (Axis, IndusInd, NESFB, SIB, Yes Bank) directed to start lending in third quarter
- All unbanked villages must receive banking coverage by December 31, 2021, with 10 villages remaining under DFS coverage
- PMEGP annual targets approved for FY 2021-22 across all districts

**Performance Highlights**
- Priority Sector Advances showed strong 63.04% positive growth despite COVID-19 impact
- ACP Priority Sector achievement declined to 30.94% in Q2 from 34.53% in previous period
- MUDRA scheme recorded year-on-year positive growth of ₹10.83 crore
- Government Sponsored Schemes disbursed ₹51.20 crores in Q2 with outstanding amount of ₹251.73 crores

**Action Items**
- HDFC Bank to immediately establish RSETI at Lunglei District by December 2021
- MzSRLM to clear pending RSETI reimbursement claims worth ₹27.38 lakh by December 31, 2021
- Banks with negative MSME growth (YES, Canara, IOB, PNB, UNI, Axis, Bandhan, Federal, IndusInd) to achieve positive growth next quarter
- All banks to conduct monthly Financial Literacy Camp meetings at rural branches as per RBI instructions

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratios below 40% (YES, Federal, SIB, Axis, NESFB, ICICI) directed to improve performance immediately
- Private banks warned of escalation to CEOs and DFS if performance in government schemes doesn't improve by March 2022
- All banks instructed to increase lending under Animal Husbandry and Fisheries through KCC scheme
- DCC meeting calendar to be prepared for all districts for next financial year

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved from 49% to 53% compared to September 2021 quarter
- ACP performance at 46.90% deemed insufficient, up from 30.94% in previous quarter
- Priority Sector Advances showed positive 15.46% year-over-year growth
- MUDRA scheme showed negative growth of Rs. 10.86 crores compared to December 2020

**Action Items**
- SLBC Convenor to ensure DCC meetings are held in 5 districts (Aizawl, Mamit, Kolasib, Serchhip, Saitual, Lawngtlai, Siaha) that had no meetings
- Government to provide banks access to digitized land records and implement legal framework reforms
- Banks with zero sanctions in various schemes (MUDRA, MSME, Priority Sector) to improve lending in Q4 FY2022

summaries.filename: VETTED SLBC MINS OF JUNE 22 QTR.pdf
summaries.source_file: mizoram/minutes/VETTED SLBC MINS OF JUNE 22 QTR.txt
summaries.summary:
**Key Decisions**
- ACP agriculture targets were revised upward as per DFS directive: MCAB (Rs. 51.91 crore), MRB (Rs. 226.02 crore), and Commercial Banks (Rs. 355.98 crore)
- DCC meetings must be held before SLBC meetings, with this becoming a mandatory rule going forward
- Letters to be sent to banks with CD ratio below 20% by the Convener Bank
- Hon'ble Chief Minister/Finance Minister to be invited to next SLBC meeting per RBI guidelines

**Performance Highlights**
- Banks achieved 23% of ACP target during Q1 FY2022-23, showing positive momentum
- CD ratio improved from 51.84% to 54.72% during June 2022 quarter
- However, 10 out of 24 banks still have CD ratio below 40%, with some banks (YES, AXIS, FED, SIB, ICICI, BANDHAN) performing particularly poorly
- MSME cluster credit remained stagnant for one year, requiring immediate attention

**Action Items**
- All banks, particularly those with low CD ratios, must improve their performance and increase priority sector lending
- LDMs to conduct pending DCC meetings for Jun'22 quarter immediately and brief forum on non-adherence to schemes
- Complete coverage of remaining 54 villages under roadmap for villages below 2000 population
- HDFC to expedite setup of long-pending RSETI at Lunglei

**Key Decisions**
- Chief Secretary warned that Agriculture, Fisheries and Animal Husbandry department secretaries must meet district-level targets or face APAR consequences, requiring review meetings before Christmas
- All banks, especially private banks, directed to increase credit linkage in government schemes like PMEGP and MUDRA with focus on maximum enrollment by next quarter
- Banks instructed to celebrate December 2nd as Single Use Plastic Free Day and display "SUP Free Office" boards in all branches

**Performance Highlights**
- Overall Annual Credit Plan (ACP) achievement at only 38%, with MSME sector performing well at 51% but Agriculture sector concerning at 21%
- CD ratio improved marginally from 54% to 56%, with Aizawl District showing lowest CD ratio of 43.15% against national benchmark of 60%
- Mizoram surpassed other regions in RBI's Financial Inclusion Index, achieving 100% banking outlet coverage for villages above 2000 population

**Action Items**
- Agriculture, Fisheries and AH&Vety department secretaries to conduct district review meetings before Christmas and report progress to Chief Secretary/SLBC
- Banks (BoB, BoM, IND, Axis, Federal, ICICI, IndusInd) to submit pending Action Taken Reports from previous meeting
- All banks to focus on increasing enrollment in social security schemes ahead of PM's review in January 2023, with special attention to non-participating banks (Bandhan, ICICI, NESFB)

**Key Decisions**
- All banks directed to maintain Credit Deposit (CD) ratio above the national benchmark of 60%, with specific focus on underperforming banks including Central Bank of India, HDFC Bank, and Punjab National Bank
- Banks with nil disbursements in agriculture sector (particularly Indian Bank, South Indian Bank, and YES Bank) instructed to immediately improve lending performance
- Priority given to achieving Annual Credit Plan targets across all sectors, especially agriculture where achievement stood at only 15.65%

**Performance Highlights**
- Mizoram achieved CD ratio of 60.79% for first time, exceeding national benchmark of 60% (increased from 58.42% in March 2024)
- Total deposits reached Rs. 17,301.17 crores with year-on-year growth of Rs. 875.20 crores; advances stood at Rs. 10,517.63 crores with growth of Rs. 1,888.12 crores
- Priority sector lending at Rs. 5,439.49 crores (51.71% of total advances) and agricultural lending at Rs. 2,218.52 crores (21.09%) both met stipulated benchmarks

**Action Items**
- Secretary of Agriculture & Horticulture to coordinate with banks to improve crop loans, KCC for animal husbandry & fisheries, and agriculture term loans
- NABARD to work with banks having zero agriculture disbursements over past three years to develop lending strategies
- All banks to focus on government flagship schemes including PMEGP, PMMY, and SHG programs, particularly in MSME sector

summaries.filename: SPECIAL SLBC MEETING FOR THE QUARTER END SEPTEMBER 2024.pdf
summaries.source_file: mizoram/minutes/SPECIAL SLBC MEETING FOR THE QUARTER END SEPTEMBER 2024.txt
summaries.summary:
**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 40% (YES Bank, Bandhan Bank, Axis Bank, ICICI Bank, CBI, Federal Bank, NESFB and HDFC Bank) must prepare roadmaps to improve above 40% and ensure controlling heads attend next meeting
- Government signed agreement with SBI, Mizoram Rural Bank and Mizoram Co-operative Apex Bank on December 18, 2024 for implementing "BANA KAIH" (Mizoram Hand holding Policy) scheme targeting agriculture and MSME sectors
- RBI to complete 100% digitization of remaining 9 districts by March 2025 (Aizawl and Serchhip already achieved 100% digitization)

**Performance Highlights**
- State CD ratio marginally crossed national benchmark of 60%, showing improvement from previous quarter
- Banking infrastructure concentrated in 3 districts: 170 out of 260 branches (65.38%) operate in Aizawl, Lunglei and Kolasib districts, leaving only 90 branches for remaining 8 districts
- RBI initiated distribution of financial literacy booklets to all 513 village libraries across Mizoram

**Action Items**
- SLBC and RBI to review banking facility availability in remote areas and improve coverage through ATMs and CSPs
- Banks to deploy adequate manpower at all branches, especially remote locations, to support customer service and BANA KAIH scheme implementation
- Line departments directed to work in close coordination with banks to improve coverage under social security schemes (PMJJBY, PMSBY, APY)

**Key Decisions**
- Chief Secretary directed banks with low CD ratios to implement improvement strategies and show appreciable figures in next meeting
- RBI requested declaration of Mizoram as 100% digitized state, having achieved 99.85% in Current Accounts and 99.95% in Savings Bank Accounts
- Letter to be sent to controlling heads requiring senior officials with policy knowledge to attend SLBC meetings
- Banks instructed to expedite pending applications for PM-Vishwakarma and PM Surya Ghar flagship schemes

**Performance Highlights**
- Annual Credit Plan achievement reached approximately 85%, though this represents a decline from 94% achieved in previous years
- Agriculture sector performance remains concerning with only 59.80% achievement and crop loan at merely 20.00%
- CD Ratio performance flagged as major concern, particularly in Aizawl and Saitual districts, with some banks showing decreasing ratios
- Only 3 banks (SBI, MRB, HDFC) currently providing KCC loans despite state having 80% small and marginal farmers

**Action Items**
- Banks with CD ratios below 40% to undergo one-on-one meetings with RBI for improvement strategies
- All banks except SBI and MRB directed to implement PM-Vishwakarma scheme and review high rejection rates
- MCAB and other banks mandated to increase agricultural lending and crop loan disbursements
- Banks to expedite processing of government flagship scheme applications pending at branch level

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 40% must improve to above 40% by next quarter with concrete actionable plans
- Sub-committee to be formed to address declining CD ratio issue with specific action plans
- SLBC to allocate targets for Medium Enterprises under MSME in consultation with relevant departments
- Village Council certified land allotments to be made acceptable by banks for KCC financing

**Performance Highlights**
- State CD ratio remains below benchmark 60%, with some banks showing sharp decline (Slice Small Finance Bank dropped from 49.53% to 25.10%)
- Agriculture sector severely underperforming with 8.41% overall achievement and only 3.20% in crop loans after one quarter against annual target of Rs. 2160 crores
- Poor performance in central schemes: high rejection rates in PM Vishwakarma, unsatisfactory PMEGP results, and PM Surya Ghar yet to take off

**Action Items**
- Complete Re-KYC campaign for PMJDY and other accounts urgently as current progress is unsatisfactory
- Conduct meaningful financial literacy and credit counseling camps (NABARD sanctioned 2,120 camps worth Rs. 1.21 crores)
- Accelerate Property Card issuance process across all districts to address non-availability of Record of Rights for loan processing

**Key Decisions**
- Government selected additional banks to join the Bana-Kaih (Hand-holding) scheme beyond the original three (SBI, MRB, MCAB) due to delays and 42% sanctioning rate
- RBI conducted special sub-committee meetings with 7 banks having CD ratios below 40%, requiring them to submit monitorable action plans for improvement
- Banks must complete Re-KYC of all accounts by March 2026 and participate in unclaimed deposit settlement campaign until December 31, 2025

**Performance Highlights**  
- State CD ratio reached 57.79%, approaching national benchmark of 60%
- Priority Sector Lending achieved 61.74% (₹7,425.24 crores), above 40% minimum requirement
- Under Government Sponsored Schemes, banks disbursed ₹2,025.01 crores to 15,334 beneficiaries during current financial year
- Bana-Kaih scheme disbursed ₹4.17 crores to 287 beneficiaries out of 680 applications

**Action Items**
- Banks with CD ratios below 40% must improve performance by end of financial year
- SLBC and RBI to review banking facility availability in remote areas beyond Aizawl, Lunglei, and Kolasib districts (which house 65.83% of 278 branches)
- All banks must address manpower shortages affecting service delivery, particularly in priority sector lending and remote areas

**Key Decisions**
- MRB directed to expedite signing of MOU with NERLP for SHG financing, with SLBC to send formal letter to push the process
- All banks mandated to follow RBI guidelines prohibiting collateral security for loans up to Rs.10 lakh in MSE sector 
- PMEGP beneficiaries must receive EDP training at RSETI for subsidy claims going forward
- Department heads required to attend meetings personally rather than sending representatives for better follow-up

**Performance Highlights**
- NRLM achievement improved significantly from 22 loans (Rs.26.90 lakhs) to 182 loans (Rs.186.00 lakhs) by December 2017, though still far from target of 1072 loans (Rs.1044 crores)
- PMEGP exceeded revised target with 629 units achieved against 473 target by March 2018
- SEP under NULM saw mixed results: 292/350 individual loans achieved, 11/100 group loans, but only 24/300 SHG loans sanctioned

**Action Items**
- MCAB & MRB to obtain auditor's certificates and resubmit interest subvention claims to NABARD
- MRB to operationalize Sakawrdai branch once V-sat installation completed; SBI to explore opening branch at Farkawn village
- All banks to sanction loan applications sourced by MzSULM and achieve 100% targets under government schemes

**Key Decisions**
- Banks must complete Aadhaar authentication of all Current & Savings Accounts by December 31, 2017 deadline using biometric, OTP-based, or demographic authentication methods
- SLBC to escalate with controlling offices of ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, Federal Bank, IndusInd Bank, Yes Bank and South Indian Bank for zero PMEGP loan sanctions in 2016-17
- Banks directed to finance Self Help Groups on "mission mode" to achieve targets, with successful SHGs to be invited to future DCC meetings to share success stories

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved slightly from 39.88% in March 2017 to 40.62% in June 2017, though Chief Secretary noted continued poor performance requiring improvement
- Deposits increased by Rs. 81.80 crores during the quarter and advances increased by Rs. 92.00 crores
- Poor achievement in government schemes: only 23 out of 1000 SHGs sanctioned under NERLP, 40 out of 1137 under MzSRLM, and only 5 out of 2000 units under NULM Self Employment Programme

**Action Items**
- MRB and NERLP to finalize MOU signing by second week of November 2017 to expedite pending SHG loan applications
- All banks to utilize NABARD's new Shallow Tube Well scheme for 2017-18 & 2018-19 to enhance agriculture finance
- LDMs to invite successful SHGs to future DCC meetings to share success stories with bank managers

**Key Decisions**
- One-day NRLM training program to be conducted for bank managers at Aizawl to coincide with next SLBC meeting, with NABARD as convener
- District-level workshops on NRLM schemes to be organized in each district with MzSRLM as convener
- All banks directed to finance eligible NULM applications sourced by MzSULM in current financial year
- MRB committed to open branches at Sakawrdai and Tuipuibari villages within the financial year

**Performance Highlights**
- NRLM credit achievement was very low with many loan applications pending, mostly with MRB branches
- PMEGP achievement reached 488 loans (74.50% of target) but margin money disbursement was only Rs.488.42 lakhs (37.26% of Rs.1310.74 lakhs target)
- NULM Self Employment Program achieved 289 units worth Rs.490.11 lakhs against target of 200 units but fell short of Rs.2000 lakhs amount target

**Action Items**
- Private banks (ICICI, HDFC, Federal, IndusInd, Yes, South Indian) to clear PMEGP loan backlogs in 2017-18
- NERLP to provide list of pending loan applications with SHG details to MRB headquarters
- Bank controlling offices to disseminate RBI instructions on NRLM interest subvention categories to rural branch managers

summaries.filename: MINUTES OF SLBC MARCH QTR '22 MEETING.pdf
summaries.source_file: mizoram/minutes/MINUTES OF SLBC MARCH QTR '22 MEETING.txt
summaries.summary:
**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratios below 40% (YES, AXIS, Federal, SIB, NESFB, ICICI & CBI) directed to improve to respectable 60% level by FY 2022-23
- All banks with negative growth in MSME sector instructed to achieve positive growth in current financial year
- Banks with zero sanctions in government schemes (AXIS, Indusind, NESFB) urged to start lending under flagship programs like MUDRA and other GSS
- All allotted banks directed to complete coverage of 90 pending unbanked villages by September 2022

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved by 3.84% in 2021-22, but 12 out of 24 banks still below 40% threshold
- Priority sector credit growth of 14.67% achieved, falling short of PM Task Force recommendation of 20% YoY growth in MSME
- ACP Priority Sector achievement reached 66.77% during FY 2021-22
- MUDRA scheme showed positive growth of Rs. 38.99 crores with total GSS disbursement of Rs. 139.26 crores

**Action Items**
- Low-performing banks to focus on priority sector lending, particularly agriculture, animal husbandry and fisheries under KCC campaigns
- Banks to improve enrollment under Social Security Schemes given low premiums and grassroots benefits
- MRB and MCAB to update village coverage status in Jandhan Rakshak Portal, with only Daido village remaining uncovered

**Key Decisions**
- All banks directed to achieve 2020-21 ACP targets in full measure; priority sector target for 2021-22 set at Rs. 863 crore
- Kohima mandated to become 100% digitally enabled district by March 2021 as per RBI/GoI directive
- Banks with CD ratio below 20% (PSB, Federal Bank, ICICI, IDBI, YES Bank) ordered to improve expeditiously
- State target of 5,000 loans under PM Street Vendors Atma Nirbhar Nidhi to be achieved

**Performance Highlights**
- PMEGP performance concerning: only 20 applications sanctioned out of 688 forwarded in September 2020
- Banks advised to focus on priority sector advances including expanded categories (solar power up to Rs. 30 crore, FPOs up to Rs. 5 crore, agri infrastructure up to Rs. 100 crore)
- State Credit Seminar conducted on 08.12.2020; Agriculture Infrastructure Fund targets allocated to 17 banks for 4-year utilization

**Action Items**
- Banks to open branches in allotted unbanked blocks without delay, with government providing infrastructure and connectivity support
- Banks must communicate rejection reasons for PMEGP applications to district nodal officers for corrective action
- All banks to step up priority sector advances and improve CD ratios; leverage state potential in handloom, bamboo handicrafts, and eco-tourism financing

**Key Decisions**
- All banks urged to improve CD ratio, particularly YES Bank, Federal Bank, Punjab & Sind Bank and ICICI Bank which had CD ratios below 20%
- Banks directed to achieve Annual Credit Plan targets by end of FY 2020-21, with special focus on agriculture sector which was only at 34.56% achievement
- Allottee banks instructed to actively pursue opening branches in un-banked blocks with government support for infrastructure and connectivity
- All banks in Kohima District mandated to ensure 100% digitization of accounts by March 2021

**Performance Highlights**
- State CD ratio improved from 44.83% in March 2020 to 46.10% in December 2020, but still below national benchmark of 60%
- MSME sector performed well at 117.73% of ACP target, while agriculture lagged significantly at only 34.56%
- Three banks (Axis Bank, Bandhan Bank, YES Bank) had zero sanctions under Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana scheme

**Action Items**
- All banks to submit reports on un-banked block branch openings and priority sector performance before next SLBC sub-committee meetings
- State government to facilitate installation of communication towers to strengthen internet connectivity for banking operations
- Bandhan Bank to confirm veracity of reported MSME disbursement figures following concerns raised by Industries & Commerce department

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratio below 20% (PSB, FED, ICICI, YES Bank) and all banks below 40% were directed to improve advance portfolios to increase the state's CD ratio from 48.44%
- Priority sector lending must reach national benchmark of 40% from current 25.31%, with agriculture portfolio to increase from 8% to 18%
- AXIS, BANDHAN, and YES Banks were urged to start sanctioning MUDRA loans as they had sanctioned zero loans by June 2021
- All banks directed to lodge Bakijai cases for NPA recovery under PMEGP and government schemes

**Performance Highlights**
- State CD ratio stands at 48.44% as of June 2021, significantly below desired levels
- Priority sector lending at only 25.31% against national benchmark of 40%
- Agriculture advances portfolio critically low at 8% versus benchmark of 18%
- Multiple banks showing zero progress on opening branches in 27 unbanked blocks despite repeated directives

**Action Items**
- All allottee banks to take concrete measures for expeditious opening of branches in unbanked blocks, with government support on infrastructure and security issues
- Banks to focus on financing allied agricultural activities and achieving ACP targets for 2021-22
- State government to support banks in expediting NPA recovery and address infrastructural challenges for new branch openings

summaries.filename: Minutes of Nagaland qtr Dec 2022 & Mar 2023.pdf
summaries.source_file: nagaland/minutes/Minutes of Nagaland qtr Dec 2022 & Mar 2023.txt
summaries.summary:
I notice that the document you've shared appears to contain only page markers ([Page 1], [Page 2], etc.) without any actual content from the SLBC meeting minutes. 

To provide you with a proper summary following your requested format with Key Decisions, Performance Highlights, and Action Items, I would need the actual text content from the Nagaland SLBC meeting minutes from December 2022.

Could you please share the document content again? This would typically include details about:
- Meeting discussions and decisions
- Banking performance data and targets
- Government scheme implementations
- Credit disbursement figures
- Action points for banks and departments

Once you provide the actual meeting content, I'll create a concise summary under 200 words with the specific sections you requested.

**Key Decisions**
- Banks with CD ratios below 40% (including IND, PSB, UCO, AXIS, BANDHAN, FED, IDFC, SIB and YES) must improve to reach RBI's 60% benchmark, with Axis Bank committing to achieve 55% by December 2023
- All controlling heads/regional heads of banks must attend SLBC meetings to ensure decision-making authority
- Banks advised to focus lending in 8 identified districts (Kiphire, Longleng, Mon, Peren, Phek, Tuensang, Wokha, Zunheboto) with lower per capita PSL to receive 125% weightage

**Performance Highlights**
- Credit growth improved with 16% YOY growth and CD ratio increased from 51.64% (June 2022) to 57.87% (June 2023), though still below 60% benchmark
- Priority Sector Lending stands at ₹2000.02 crores but constitutes only 21.88% of total advances, significantly below national 40% benchmark
- PMEGP Gross NPA improved from 26.18% (March 2023) to 18.62%, though recovery remains challenging

**Action Items**
- Banks to lodge certificate cases for PMEGP loan recovery with state government support for expediting recovery processes
- Increase participation in Atal Pension Yojana (achieved only 3063 of 15500 target by August 2023) and implement new PM Vishwakarma scheme launched September 2023
- Bandhan and HDFC banks to resolve technical issues and process pending NULM applications by November 2023

**Key Decisions**
- State government directed that Priority Lending Program (PLP) target for FY 2024-25 must be set at 40% of current total advances (Rs 9,970 Cr) and will not approve NABARD's proposed lower target until reviewed with all stakeholders
- State government set specific lending targets: 1,000 education loans, 2,000 skill development loans, and Rs 2,000 Cr for MSME with government providing CGTMSE guarantee fee
- Banks with CD ratio below 40% (IND, PSB, AXIS, BANDHAN, FED, IDBI, IDFC, SIB, YES) were directed to improve performance to reach RBI's 60% benchmark
- Principal Secretary expressed displeasure at banks not sending Regional Heads/Controllers to SLBC meetings despite previous directives

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved significantly from 52.26% in March 2023 to 61.15% in December 2023, crossing RBI's 60% benchmark for the first time
- Total ACP target achievement reached 89.25% by December 2023
- Priority Sector Lending at Rs 2,107.62 Cr represents only 21.13% of total advances, falling short of RBI's 40% benchmark

**Action Items**
- All banks to expedite opening branches in 22 unbanked blocks and establish brick & mortar branches in villages with 3,000+ population
- Banks to clear pending applications under CMMFI scheme and reduce high NPAs in PMEGP and NULM schemes
- SLBC to create dedicated website for Nagaland and establish secretariat for financial inclusion as per RBI's Lead Bank Scheme

**Key Decisions**
- Finance Commissioner to establish dedicated banking vertical within Finance Department for daily bank interactions and issue resolution
- Chief Minister to convene Credit Summit to launch ACP 2024-25, Modified CMMFI policy guidelines, and Universal Life Insurance Scheme
- Government recruited Credit Managers and Credit Coordinators in each district through SIDBI collaboration
- Banks warned of potential account closure and business withdrawal if they don't participate in CMMFI implementation

**Performance Highlights**
- Credit-Deposit ratio declined from 61.85% to 57.58% in March 2024, below 60% benchmark target
- Overall ACP achievement reached 118.10%, but Agriculture sector performed poorly at only 51.64%
- Priority sector lending at Rs 2,333.54 Cr (22.39% of advances) fell significantly short of 40% benchmark
- High NPA rates in government schemes: PMEGP at 49.25% and NULM at 17.75%

**Action Items**
- All banks to maintain 60% CD ratio throughout FY 2024-25 with quarterly monitoring
- LDMs to conduct Pre-PLP meetings involving line departments, NABARD, RBI and banks
- Banks to establish branches in 22 unbanked blocks and focus expansion beyond four concentrated districts (Dimapur, Kohima, Chumoukedima, Mokokchung)
- Action Taken Reports to be submitted by August 31, 2024

**Key Decisions**
- NABARD announced the Potential Linked Credit Plan for FY 2026-27 totaling Rs 3,088 crore for Nagaland
- Priority sector lending target maintained at 28% of total advances by end of FY 2025-26, as previously approved
- RBI directed banks to implement RSETI opening decisions on priority basis without delays based on lead bank considerations
- State government emphasized need to finalize Annual Credit Plan (ACP) for FY 2026-27 within prescribed timeline

**Performance Highlights**
- CD Ratio improved to 63.64% as of September 2025, up from 59.98% in March 2025, though Kohima district remains low at 32.68%
- Priority sector lending achievement stood at only 39.56% of annual target as of September 2025
- Multiple banks reported nil performance in agriculture and MSME priority sectors, including IDFC, INDUS, YES, ESAF, and SSFB
- Only one RSETI operational in Peren district out of 17 districts statewide

**Action Items**
- LDM Kohima to review CD ratio commitments from 17 bank branches below 40% threshold in next sub-committee meeting
- Concerned banks to increase focus on priority sector advances, particularly in agriculture and MSME segments
- PSB to submit action plan for resolving portal-related issues preventing education loan sanctions

**Key Decisions**
- All banks must achieve ACP (Annual Credit Plan) targets for FY 2021-22 by March 2022
- Banks with CD ratio below 10% (Punjab & Sind Bank, IDFC First Bank, Yes Bank, Federal Bank) must show substantial improvement by March 2022
- SARFAESI Act 2002 implementation in Nagaland effective December 10, 2021; banks to maintain trend analysis and present data at next meeting
- Two new RSETI proposals (Phek and Dimapur) to be examined in sub-committee meeting

**Performance Highlights**
- CD ratio improved from 48.44% (June 2021) to 51.88% (December 2021), but still below RBI benchmark of 60%
- Priority sector advances under ACP achieved only 57% by December 2021
- Several banks (HDFC, IDFC, ICICI, YES Bank, etc.) had NIL outstanding in government sponsored schemes

**Action Items**
- Allottee banks to establish CSP/BCs in all 27 unbanked blocks by March 31, 2022
- Rural Development Department to provide correct longitude/latitude data for 84 villages by March 31, 2022 for DFS DBT-GIS Portal mapping
- SLBC and NRLM to coordinate resolution of pending RSETI training expense reimbursement claims in Peren

**Key Decisions**
- Establishment of 14 functional MSME clusters across Nagaland under two major schemes: 5 clusters under MSE-Common Development Programme (CDP) and 9 clusters under Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries (SFURTI)
- Focus on food processing sector with 8 clusters dedicated to various food processing activities including honey, spices, pineapple, turmeric, ginger, kiwi, and mushroom processing
- Strategic distribution across 7 districts with Dimapur, Mokokchung, and Zunheboto receiving multiple cluster allocations

**Performance Highlights**
- Comprehensive coverage of traditional industries including bamboo & cane crafts, wooden furniture, and Naga tribal jewellery manufacturing
- Diversified cluster portfolio spanning agriculture, food processing, handicrafts, and animal feed production
- Strong emphasis on value addition in agricultural products and traditional crafts

**Action Items**
- MSME Development & Facilitation Office to coordinate implementation of all 14 clusters as communicated to RBI
- Banks to provide necessary financing support for Common Facility Centers (CFCs) establishment in each cluster
- District-wise monitoring and facilitation of cluster development activities across Dimapur, Mokokchung, Tuensang, Zunheboto, Kiphire, Phek, and Chumoukedima
